items.q: What is the difference between CAD and CAM?
items.correct: CAD creates digital designs; CAM uses those designs to control manufacturing machines.
items.misconception: They are the same thing with different names.
items.random_wrong: CAD is for cars, CAM is for cameras.
items.why: Students treat the terms as synonymous because they often appear together. CAD = drawing/modelling software; CAM = machine control (CNC routers, 3D printers) - typically the second stage.

items.correct: A drawing showing multiple 2D views (front, side, plan) of an object.
items.misconception: A 3D drawing showing the object at an angle.
items.random_wrong: A drawing in black ink only.
items.why: Students confuse orthographic (multi-view 2D) with isometric (single 3D view). Orthographic projects each face flat onto separate views - used for engineering specification.

items.q: What is the difference between a wood stain and a varnish?
items.correct: Stain colours the wood; varnish provides a protective surface coat.
items.random_wrong: Stain is permanent, varnish always peels off.
items.why: Students confuse colouring (stain penetrates) with protecting (varnish sits on top). Many products combine both, but their primary functions differ. Often stain is applied first, then varnish.
It's FDM 3D printer. “CNC router” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
Correct answer: Liquid resin with UV. “Filament” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: A 3D print uses 24 g of PLA at £0.05/g. What is the material cost (£)?
items.why: The real figure is 1.2. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
